critic_raw:
CRITIQUE:

C1 — ACA RATING FACTOR CONFLICT: Age and region alongside other ML features could produce effective premium differentiation beyond permissible ACA factors.

C2 — HEALTH STATUS PROXY: Prior-year claims is a health status proxy. ACA restricts health status as a rating factor.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE (responding to critic):

C1 — ACA community rating rules apply to member-facing premiums. This model is for internal cost prediction. Outside counsel confirmed the distinction. No community rating violation.

C2 — ACA health status restriction applies to individual premium determination, not internal reserve modeling. The model never determines individual premiums. Prior-year claims is appropriate for actuarial reserve purposes.
adjudication_raw: Defense wins. The committee's concerns are scope errors — they apply premium rating regulations to an internal actuarial model. The outside counsel review establishes this clearly.
